title: He’s running the Boston Marathon for the ‘amazing’ Make-A-Wish children
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Jason Perry from Stockton, California, will run the 2026 Boston Marathon for Make-A-Wish Greater Massachusetts-Rhode Island.
Perry's participation aims to honor and support wish children, providing hope and raising funds for their causes.
Over 15 years, Perry has granted wishes for many children, dedicating his marathon run to their inspiration and memory.

title: Insider predicts Patriots sign Seahawks standout pass rusher, but don’t land Alec Pierce in free agency
author: Conor Roche
bullets: ESPN insider Dan Graziano predicts the Patriots will sign Seahawks edge rusher Boye Mafe to a three-year, $48 million deal.
Mafe recorded 44 pressures with a 17.4% pass rush win rate last season, a top stat compared to Patriots' current roster.
Graziano suggests Colts wide receiver Alec Pierce will sign with Chargers, not Patriots, likely with a three-year, $80 million contract.

title: Review & setlist: With sharp sarcasm, Jesse Welles took on war, bullies, and Trump at the House of Blues
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Jesse Welles performed at the House of Blues in Boston on March 6, 2026, as part of his 'Under the Powerlines Tour'.
The Arkansas native's setlist included socially and politically charged songs, alongside covers of Black Sabbath and Radiohead tracks.
Welles's performance featured sharp sarcasm in his commentary on war, politics, and media, engaging the crowd throughout the night.

title: Masataka Yoshida hits a home run in Japan’s win vs. Korea, with Drake Maye in attendance
author: Conor Roche
bullets: Masataka Yoshida hit a home run and drove in three runs in Japan's 8-6 win over Korea in the World Baseball Classic.
Yoshida went 2-for-4, helping Japan to lead 2-0 in pool play with additional performances against Chinese Taipei.
Patriots quarterback Drake Maye attended the game at the Tokyo Dome, as part of his visit to Japan with his wife.

title: This Roslindale native is running her first Boston Marathon for Mass. Eye and Ear
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Makayla Aboujaoude-Quirk, a 25-year-old from Roslindale, will run her first Boston Marathon for Team Eye and Ear.
Aboujaoude-Quirk is running to raise funds for Mass Eye and Ear, where she has been a patient for 10 years.
Mass Eye and Ear has Massachusetts' only 24-hour emergency department for specialized eye, neck, ear, nose, and throat trauma.

title: It was overdue, but Tim Legler was promoted to ESPN/ABC’s top NBA broadcast team this season, and he’s thriving
author: Chad Finn
bullets: Tim Legler promoted to ESPN/ABC's top NBA broadcast team, joining Mike Breen and Richard Jefferson for game coverage.
Legler, with ESPN since 2000, plays key analyst role in team's coverage of Celtics games and the NBA Finals.
Legler commented on Celtics' players and strategy, highlighting Jaylen Brown's growth and Jayson Tatum's return.

title: Cooper Flagg was the Maine story in Jayson Tatum’s return
author: Chad Finn
bullets: Jayson Tatum returned to the Celtics 298 days after an Achilles injury, scoring 15 points with 12 rebounds and 7 assists.
Mavericks rookie Cooper Flagg had 16 points, 8 rebounds, and 6 assists during his TD Garden debut in a 120-100 loss to the Celtics.
Flagg, a Maine native and Tatum admirer, received a warm Boston crowd reception, likened to a conference finals atmosphere.

title: Charles Barkley impressed by Jayson Tatum in return, but doesn’t view Celtics as favorites in East
author: Conor Roche
bullets: Jayson Tatum returns to the Celtics after 298 days post-Achilles tear, contributing 15 points, 12 rebounds, and 7 assists.
Charles Barkley acknowledges Tatum's performance but maintains the Cavaliers as Eastern Conference favorites after acquiring James Harden.
Boston Celtics lead the NBA in wins since January 1, tying with the Spurs at 22 victories for the period.

title: Mass. man accused of impersonating an Army veteran for more than 30 years
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: James D. Sommers of Pittsfield accused of impersonating a U.S. Army veteran since the 1990s.
Sommers allegedly collected thousands in benefits and $30,000 in VA medical care under stolen identity.
Sommers faces up to five years in prison for false statements, with multiple prior convictions under the victim's name.

title: Watch: Body cam video shows officer rescuing man, 78, from burning truck
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Easton police and bystanders rescued a 78-year-old man from a burning truck on Route 138 after a crash.
Patrol Officer Dean Soucie and helpers pulled Francis Leverone from the engulfed vehicle, saving him from serious injuries.
The crash involved a disabled dump truck, Leverone's Ford F-350, and a Toyota Camry; investigation is ongoing.
